coaching.coaching: Plan how verification method for AC1 and AC2 will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification method for AC1 and AC2

coaching.coaching: Pin down credential-helper REST pattern example with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: credential-helper REST pattern example

coaching.coaching: Plan how documentation checks referenced in AC3 will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: documentation checks referenced in AC3

coaching.coaching: Realign leaf scope versus parent epic boundary to the parent's plan — the parent wins on conflict; if the parent is genuinely wrong, update the PARENT first (which forces its re-review), never silently diverge the leaf.
coaching.subject: leaf scope versus parent epic boundary

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] existing secret-redaction logic in AGENTS.md will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: existing secret-redaction logic in AGENTS.md

coaching.coaching: Run a quick pre-mortem on risks in under-specified acceptance criteria: how could this plan fail?
coaching.subject: risks in under-specified acceptance criteria

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific test file(s) that will be updated or created to enforce the AC1/AC2 content (e.g., extend `tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py` to assert `git credential fill` and netrc-401 strings appear in AGENTS.md).
evidence: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py: asserts 'recent merged Gerrit history', 'feature-branch-driver', 'git push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main', 'dependency order', 'LLM-Review +1', 'Verified +1', 'ADR 0025', 'not an implementation blocker' — no credential-fill or netrc strings
Plan body does not name any test that will enforce AC1 or AC2 text requirements
findings.finding: AC3 states 'Documentation checks pass' but the plan never names which checks cover the new credential-helper/netrc/401 content. The only existing Codex-guidance test (tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py) asserts a fixed set of strings ('feature-branch-driver', 'LLM-Review +1', etc.) that do not include 'git credential fill', 'netrc', '/a/', or any 401-explanation text. Whether the new AC1/AC2 content will be gated by a new or updated test is left entirely implicit.
scenarios: Executor adds the prose to AGENTS.md, runs the existing test suite, sees test_codex_agent_guidance.py pass (it checks different strings), and closes the ticket — leaving AC1/AC2 content unguarded against future edits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py` as an edit target and specify what new assertion(s) it should gain to cover the credential-helper REST pattern and the netrc-401 clarification.
evidence: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py directly reads AGENTS.md and asserts on specific strings in the Codex Gerrit workflow rule section (lines 10–19).
The plan proposes adding 'the safe credential-helper REST pattern' to the same Codex Gerrit section but names no test update.
AC3 says 'Documentation checks pass' without specifying that this includes the existing test assertions or that the test needs new assertions covering the new content.
findings.finding: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py is a direct consumer of the **Codex Gerrit workflow rule:** block in AGENTS.md that the plan proposes to extend with credential-helper REST content, but the plan does not enumerate this file as an edit target or acknowledge that its assertions may need updating to cover the new guidance.
findings.location: Plan section: Plan body / AC1; file: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py
scenarios: An implementer adds the new credential-fill guidance to AGENTS.md but does not add corresponding assertions to test_codex_agent_guidance.py; the new content is unguarded and can drift silently.

The implementer inserts the new content inside the exact block the test delimits (between '**Codex Gerrit workflow rule:' and '## Record your work'), causing test_index errors if the section boundaries shift.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second test function (or extend the existing one) in test_codex_agent_guidance.py that asserts `'git credential fill'` and a key phrase distinguishing the netrc-401 explanation (e.g. `'curl --netrc'` or `'--netrc 401'`) appear in the AGENTS.md Codex section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Re-parent this leaf under a ticket whose scope covers Codex contributor documentation / Gerrit authentication UX, OR create a new parent ticket for that scope and update this leaf's `parent_id` before proceeding — the current parent (jira-reb-1160) covers plan-review attestation infrastructure only and does not contain any documentation deliverable about Gerrit REST credentials.
evidence: Leaf Goal: 'Prevent Codex epic executions from misdiagnosing working Gerrit credentials when REST polling uses curl.'

Leaf Plan: 'Add lean Codex-specific contributor guidance that Gerrit HTTPS credentials stored by the configured Git credential helper must be obtained through git credential fill for authenticated /a/ REST calls; curl --netrc is not an equivalent check unless .netrc was explicitly configured.'
Leaf AC1: 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc states the safe credential-helper REST pattern for non-interactive Codex.'
Leaf AC2: 'The example never prints the password and explains that a --netrc 401 does not invalidate credentials already working for git-over-HTTPS.'
Leaf file_impact targets AGENTS.md and CONTRIBUTING.md.
Parent jira-reb-1160 title: 'Keep plan-review attestations current across relationships and execution' — attestation machinery only.

Parent scope covers: signed plan-material pins, plan_review_phase projection, execution-review compatibility, close-gate enforcement, prerequisite consistency criterion, derived health — zero documentation deliverables.
Parent has 22 ACs, none of which address Gerrit authentication, credential helpers, AGENTS.md content, curl, or contributor UX guidance.

ABSENCE: The parent's 'Not in scope' list and full description (~5000 words of design) contain zero references to Gerrit credentials, curl, git credential fill, AGENTS.md documentation additions, or contributor guidance about REST authentication.
findings.finding: The leaf's declared scope — adding Gerrit REST credential-helper guidance to AGENTS.md/CONTRIBUTING.md — is entirely outside the parent epic's scope, which is limited to plan-review attestation infrastructure (signed pins, plan_review_phase projection, execution-review compatibility, close gates, prerequisite consistency, derived health). The parent's plan, 22 ACs, and 'Not in scope' list contain no mention of Gerrit credentials, curl --netrc, git credential fill, non-interactive Codex authentication, or any contributor documentation deliverable.
findings.location: Leaf plan (Goal, Plan, Acceptance Criteria) vs. parent jira-reb-1160 scope and ACs
scenarios: An implementer completing this leaf believes they are fulfilling a parent deliverable when in fact no parent deliverable covers this work — the leaf is orphaned from its parent's contract.
The parent epic closes without any of its 22 ACs requiring this documentation, leaving the leaf's work unanchored in the dependency chain.
A plan-review gate that checks leaf-parent containment will block this leaf, stalling work that may be valid but is attached to the wrong parent.
findings.suggested_fix: Create a new standalone task or story (or find an existing contributor-docs epic) that covers 'Improve Codex contributor guidance for non-interactive Gerrit authentication' and re-parent this leaf under it via `rebar link be4e-8716-87bf-4dd0 <new-parent-id> parent`. If no suitable parent exists, this leaf can be promoted to a standalone task with no parent rather than remaining incorrectly parented under jira-reb-1160.

evidence: no verification commands or testing plan referenced
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define in the plan body what 'documentation checks' means (e.g., markdownlint CI step, a specific make target, or manual review gate) so AC3 is verifiable.
evidence: 'Documentation checks pass' appears only in the AC list
Plan body describes only adding text to AGENTS.md; no CI/lint/check mechanism is mentioned anywhere
findings.finding: AC3 ('Documentation checks pass') references checks that are never described in the plan body — the plan does not specify what documentation checks exist, how they are invoked (e.g., markdownlint, CI pipeline step, manual review), or where they live, leaving this criterion unmappable to described work.
scenarios: An implementer writes the documentation, runs no checks, and cannot determine whether AC3 is satisfied.
A reviewer cannot verify AC3 without guessing at what 'documentation checks' means.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Plan section such as: 'Documentation checks are satisfied by running `markdownlint AGENTS.md` (or the repo's existing doc-lint CI step) with zero errors.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC1 and AC2 (e.g., `grep -E 'git credential fill' AGENTS.md` for AC1 and `grep -c 'password' AGENTS.md` asserting 0 matches for AC2) so done-ness is reproducibly evidenced.
evidence: AC1: 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc states the safe credential-helper REST pattern for non-interactive Codex.'
AC2: 'The example never prints the password and explains that a --netrc 401 does not invalidate credentials already working for git-over-HTTPS.'
No grep, diff, or file-inspection command appears anywhere in the plan for these two criteria.
findings.finding: AC1 and AC2 have no concrete proving command — the plan never specifies how an agent confirms the required text is present in the target file (e.g., a grep pattern or file-review step), leaving verification implicit.
scenarios: An agent adds documentation that omits the key explanation or includes password output; without a specified check, the AC is satisfied by a subjective read rather than a reproducible command.
Reviewer cannot replay verification — 'done' is unauditable.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to each AC a parenthetical proving command:
- AC1: `(prove: grep -qE 'git credential fill' AGENTS.md && echo PASS)`
- AC2: `(prove: grep -qvE 'password\s*=' AGENTS.md && grep -q '401 does not invalidate' AGENTS.md && echo PASS)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and state explicitly whether secret-redaction guidance already exists in AGENTS.md (or CONTRIBUTING.md); if it does not, replace 'Preserve secret redaction' with 'Add secret-redaction guidance' so the plan's premise matches reality.
evidence: AGENTS.md grep for 'redact', 'credential', 'curl', 'netrc': zero matches
infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh line 99–103: 'NEVER echoed' pattern exists there, not in AGENTS.md
src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py line 14: 'any token/secret redaction stays where it already lives' — but that is gitutil.py, not AGENTS.md
findings.finding: The plan says 'Preserve secret redaction' as if secret-redaction logic already exists in AGENTS.md (or the target contributor doc) and must not be disturbed. Probing AGENTS.md reveals zero mentions of redaction, credential, curl, or netrc — the redaction pattern exists in infra/gerrit scripts (feature-branch-e2e.sh) and src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py, not in AGENTS.md. The plan's descoping claim ('preserve what's already there') thus rests on an unverified codebase assertion about the target file.
findings.location: Plan body: 'Preserve secret redaction and keep normal git push guidance unchanged.'
scenarios: An implementer reads 'preserve secret redaction' in AGENTS.md context, finds nothing to preserve, and either skips the redaction requirement or adds duplicate/conflicting text, because the plan's premise is false for the target file.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Preserve secret redaction and keep normal git push guidance unchanged.' with: 'Add a note that the password must never be printed (mirroring the NEVER-echoed convention in infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh); keep the existing Git workflow section in AGENTS.md unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a single authoritative target file in the plan (e.g., 'Add the guidance to `AGENTS.md`; if a separate contributor doc is the canonical location, name it explicitly') rather than leaving it as a disjunction.
evidence: 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc' — the disjunction defers a real file-selection decision to the executor.
If the repo has both AGENTS.md and a separate CONTRIBUTING.md, the agent must guess which is authoritative.
findings.finding: The target file is stated as 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc', leaving the executor to choose which file to modify — a defaultable gap that should be resolved in the plan.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Add lean Codex-specific contributor guidance'; AC1 — 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc'
scenarios: Agent edits CONTRIBUTING.md; reviewer expects AGENTS.md; AC1 is disputed as satisfied or not.
Two agents working on related tasks edit different files, producing duplicate or conflicting guidance.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc' with the definitive filename (e.g., `AGENTS.md`) throughout the plan and ACs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or expand AC3) to verify that existing git push guidance in AGENTS.md is preserved unchanged after the edit.
evidence: 'keep normal git push guidance unchanged' is stated in the Plan body
None of AC1, AC2, or AC3 verifies that existing git push guidance remains intact after the edit
findings.finding: The plan body states 'keep normal git push guidance unchanged' as a constraint but no acceptance criterion verifies that the existing git push guidance was not accidentally modified, leaving this described deliverable uncovered.
findings.location: Plan section — 'Preserve secret redaction and keep normal git push guidance unchanged'
scenarios: An implementer inadvertently reformats or trims surrounding content in AGENTS.md, and there is no AC to catch the regression.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3 to: 'Documentation checks pass and a diff of AGENTS.md shows no modification to existing git push guidance sections.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the ACs — e.g., a worked dogfooding example or a before/after scenario showing that the documented pattern correctly distinguishes a working credential from a curl --netrc 401 — so the improvement to the named beneficiary is verifiable.
evidence: AC3: 'Documentation checks pass' — this is a mechanical linting or CI format check, not a workflow or operational validation.

AC2 describes content requirements ('example never prints the password and explains that a --netrc 401 does not invalidate credentials') but provides no mechanism to verify the content achieves the stated goal of preventing misdiagnosis.
F4 checklist: 'At least one criterion carries a concrete validation mechanism (before/after workflow comparison, an operational metric target, dogfooding).'
findings.finding: No criterion carries a concrete validation mechanism that verifies the documentation actually prevents misdiagnosis. AC3 ('Documentation checks pass') is a lint/CI gate on formatting, not a before/after workflow comparison or dogfooding step showing that a Codex execution no longer misdiagnoses credentials after reading the guidance.
scenarios: A Codex contributor reads the documentation, but because there is no scenario-based validation (e.g., a sample run showing the correct git credential fill flow succeeding where curl --netrc previously gave a false 401), the guidance may still be misunderstood or incomplete, and the original misdiagnosis problem recurs undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A worked example in the doc demonstrates: (a) `git credential fill` returning credentials, (b) a curl call using those credentials succeeding against /a/ REST, and (c) an explicit note that a curl --netrc 401 is not evidence of credential failure when git-over-HTTPS works — reviewable by a Codex contributor as a smoke-test during onboarding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the Plan body explaining that AGENTS.md is the required target because Codex loads it at session start (per `.codex/config.toml` and the `@`-import model), and update AC1 to require AGENTS.md specifically rather than 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc'.
evidence: Plan body: 'Add lean Codex-specific contributor guidance' — states *what* but not *why AGENTS.md*
AC1: 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc' — the hedge shows the author is uncertain which file is correct
AGENTS.md line 10: 'an @-import loads at launch and does not reduce context' — the key reason AGENTS.md is the right vehicle is not cited in the plan
.codex/config.toml: project_doc_fallback_filenames = ["AGENTS.md"] — confirms Codex reads AGENTS.md at startup, not CONTRIBUTING.md
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why AGENTS.md (rather than CONTRIBUTING.md or another doc) is the correct vehicle for this guidance. This is load-bearing: AC1 hedges with 'AGENTS.md or the authoritative contributor doc', leaving the implementer free to choose CONTRIBUTING.md — which Codex does NOT read at session launch — making the fix ineffective for the stated beneficiary.
scenarios: An implementer puts the guidance in CONTRIBUTING.md (a plausible 'authoritative contributor doc'); Codex never loads it at session start, so the misdiagnosis continues unaffected.
A reviewer accepts the plan as satisfied regardless of which file is chosen, because the AC permits either — leaving the goal unmet.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Plan section add one sentence: 'AGENTS.md is the required file because Codex loads it at every session start (`.codex/config.toml: project_doc_fallback_filenames = ["AGENTS.md"]`), making it the only doc that steers non-interactive Codex without requiring the agent to navigate to it.' Then tighten AC1 to: 'AGENTS.md states the safe credential-helper REST pattern for non-interactive Codex.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
